result:
1. Trusts client `X-Forwarded-For` for rate-limit keying
evidence: `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip` in diff; Section 2 rule 2; SEV1 2025-09 same bug
impact: attacker rotates XFF per request, bypasses per-IP limit, enables brute-force/credential-stuffing
fix: key off `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`; fall back to `req.socket.remoteAddress`; never read `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']` here — `no-direct-xff-read` lint rule already forbids it
verify: add unit test sending forged `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4` from fixed socket, assert counter keyed on socket IP; `pnpm lint` must flag the current line

2. `INCR` without `EXPIRE` — unbounded Redis keys
evidence: `await redis.incr(key)` with no paired `expire`; Section 4 code sample; SEV2 2025-11 Redis OOM same bug
impact: keys accumulate forever under traffic, Redis hits maxmemory, eviction storm knocks edge offline
fix: wrap in `redis.multi().incr(key).expire(key, 90).exec()` per Section 4; `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint rule should catch this
verify: integration test asserting `TTL key` > 0 after first increment; load test confirms key count bounded

3. Missing route prefix + region in key — cross-route/cross-region collision
evidence: key is `rl:${ip}` only; handbook spec is `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` with region per SEV2 2025-12
impact: one busy route/region exhausts limit for unrelated routes and other regions; spurious `429`s, and a single global budget instead of per-route/per-region
fix: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}`; pull region from env, bucket from `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`
verify: regression test from 2025-12 RCA; assert keys differ across regions and routes for same IP

4. No limit comparison / no 429 response
evidence: diff increments but never reads count, never compares to limit, never returns RFC 7807 `429`
impact: limiter is effectively a counter only — no throttling actually happens; SLO + abuse controls silently broken
fix: capture `[count] = await multi.exec()`, compare against per-route limit from `config/rate_limits.json`, return problem+json body with `type:/errors/rate-limited`, `retry_after`
verify: unit test exceeding limit returns `429` with RFC 7807 shape and `retry_after` seconds

5. No error handling on Redis call
evidence: bare `await redis.incr` — transient Redis failure throws, likely surfaces as `5xx`
impact: Redis blip fails open or fails closed unpredictably; metric `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action=errored}` not emitted
fix: try/catch, emit `action=errored` metric, use `src/util/retry.ts` (≤3 attempts, exp backoff) for transient errors, fail-open with logged warning per team convention
verify: inject Redis connection error in test; assert metric increment and request still served

raw_payload.result:
1. Trusts client `X-Forwarded-For` for rate-limit keying
evidence: `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip` in diff; Section 2 rule 2; SEV1 2025-09 same bug
impact: attacker rotates XFF per request, bypasses per-IP limit, enables brute-force/credential-stuffing
fix: key off `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`; fall back to `req.socket.remoteAddress`; never read `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']` here — `no-direct-xff-read` lint rule already forbids it
verify: add unit test sending forged `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4` from fixed socket, assert counter keyed on socket IP; `pnpm lint` must flag the current line

2. `INCR` without `EXPIRE` — unbounded Redis keys
evidence: `await redis.incr(key)` with no paired `expire`; Section 4 code sample; SEV2 2025-11 Redis OOM same bug
impact: keys accumulate forever under traffic, Redis hits maxmemory, eviction storm knocks edge offline
fix: wrap in `redis.multi().incr(key).expire(key, 90).exec()` per Section 4; `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint rule should catch this
verify: integration test asserting `TTL key` > 0 after first increment; load test confirms key count bounded

3. Missing route prefix + region in key — cross-route/cross-region collision
evidence: key is `rl:${ip}` only; handbook spec is `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` with region per SEV2 2025-12
impact: one busy route/region exhausts limit for unrelated routes and other regions; spurious `429`s, and a single global budget instead of per-route/per-region
fix: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}`; pull region from env, bucket from `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`
verify: regression test from 2025-12 RCA; assert keys differ across regions and routes for same IP

4. No limit comparison / no 429 response
evidence: diff increments but never reads count, never compares to limit, never returns RFC 7807 `429`
impact: limiter is effectively a counter only — no throttling actually happens; SLO + abuse controls silently broken
fix: capture `[count] = await multi.exec()`, compare against per-route limit from `config/rate_limits.json`, return problem+json body with `type:/errors/rate-limited`, `retry_after`
verify: unit test exceeding limit returns `429` with RFC 7807 shape and `retry_after` seconds

5. No error handling on Redis call
evidence: bare `await redis.incr` — transient Redis failure throws, likely surfaces as `5xx`
impact: Redis blip fails open or fails closed unpredictably; metric `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action=errored}` not emitted
fix: try/catch, emit `action=errored` metric, use `src/util/retry.ts` (≤3 attempts, exp backoff) for transient errors, fail-open with logged warning per team convention
verify: inject Redis connection error in test; assert metric increment and request still served
